channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Fuel Supplies in Crimea, Creating Shortages on Russian-Held Peninsula
item.description: Ukrainian forces targeted fuel supplies to Crimea, causing a fuel crisis on the occupied peninsula in a new blow to Moscow's war narrative.

item.title: European Bonds Rally as Trump Touts Progress on Iran Peace Deal
item.description: European bonds surged while oil prices slid on renewed prospects for a Middle East peace deal, with Trump indicating an agreement could be reached soon.

item.title: Ebola Outbreak Spreads in Congo as Cracked Coffin Traced to Super-Spreader Event
item.description: A pastor's coffin that broke on the rocky road to his burial is suspected as one of the earliest super-spreader events in Congo's growing Ebola epidemic.

item.title: Ukraine and Russia Exchange Overnight Drone Strikes, Targeting Energy and Rail Systems
item.description: Russia and Ukraine carried out far-reaching drone strikes overnight, with Ukraine hitting a major oil processing region while Russia targeted railway stations and electrical substations.

item.title: Iran war deal close as Strait of Hormuz tensions linger
item.description: Hopes for peace grew after Trump said a deal could be signed this weekend, though Tehran said no final decision has been made on a pact. The strategic Strait of Hormuz remains a key sticking point in negotiations.

item.title: US and EU near deadline on $11.5 billion tariffs in decades-long aircraft dispute
item.description: Washington and Brussels have yet to decide whether to continue suspending or reimpose tariffs on $11.5 billion of goods in a dispute over aircraft subsidies, with their truce expiring within days.

item.title: UK Defense Minister Resigns Over Defense Funding
item.description: UK Armed Forces Minister Al Carns resigned in protest over lack of defense funding, following Defense Secretary John Healey's earlier resignation. Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces a political crisis as two defense ministers quit within days.

item.title: Philippines Defense Chief Vows to Press on Despite Chinese Sanctions
item.description: Philippine Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro vowed to continue his duties despite China sanctioning him for "erroneous remarks," calling out China's "wickedness" as tensions escalate over South China Sea disputes.

item.title: UAE Sends Military Planes to South Korea for Missile Defense Transport
item.description: The United Arab Emirates dispatched military aircraft to South Korea to expedite transport of Korean-made Cheongung missile defense systems, highlighting deepening defense cooperation.

item.title: ECB signals option to raise rates again in July after insurance hike
item.description: The European Central Bank raised interest rates for the first time in nearly three years, with Bundesbank President Nagel saying all options remain open for July to combat Iran-war-driven energy inflation.

item.title: UK lags European rivals in curbing influx of cheap parcels from China
item.description: More cheap parcels are flowing into the UK from China than into rival European economies, undermining British retailers and raising concerns about the UKs ability to address the trade imbalance.

item.title: World chess body suspends Russia after sports tribunal ruling
item.description: The International Chess Federation suspended Russia following a Court of Arbitration for Sport ruling in favor of Ukraine, ending decades of Russian dominance in the game.

item.title: Disaster drills helped prevent more deaths in powerful Philippine earthquake
item.description: Philippine officials say years of disaster-preparedness drills helped prevent a larger casualty toll when a powerful magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck the southern Philippines.

item.title: US Plans Major Cuts to Fighter Jets and Warships for NATO Operations in Europe
item.description: The United States plans to significantly reduce aircraft and warships available for NATO operations in Europe, according to a New York Times report citing senior European officials. The move raises concerns about US commitment to European security.

item.title: British Defence Minister John Healey Resigns Over Spending Dispute
item.description: UK Defence Minister John Healey quit on Thursday, accusing Prime Minister Keir Starmer of failing to commit the resources needed to keep Britain safe from mounting threats. The resignation highlights tensions over military spending.

item.title: China Cancels High-Level Diplomatic Meetings With EU in Beijing
item.description: China cancelled at short notice two diplomatic meetings planned with the European Union in Beijing this month, the Financial Times reported, raising questions about the state of EU-China relations.

item.title: Fuel Stations in Crimea Run Dry After Ukrainian Drone Strikes
item.description: Fuel stations on the Russian-held Crimean peninsula were out of petrol on Thursday as a Ukrainian campaign against supply lines escalates, highlighting the effectiveness of Ukrainian strikes on Russian logistics.

item.title: Geneva Steps Up Security for 50,000-Strong G7 Protest
item.description: Swiss authorities are preparing for a massive anti-G7 protest expected this weekend in Geneva as France hosts the Group of Seven summit. Shopkeepers have boarded up businesses ahead of the demonstration.

item.title: Japan-South Korea Summit Aims to Deepen Cooperation Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi and South Korean President Lee Jae Myung held a summit on May 19, discussing enhanced cooperation in an increasingly volatile geopolitical environment.

item.title: France, UK, Germany Envoys Meet Russian Foreign Ministry Officials
item.description: Ambassadors from France, the UK, and Germany arrived at the Russian Foreign Ministry for meetings with senior diplomats, amid heightened tensions over the Ukraine war.

item.title: Germany and France Scrap FCAS Fighter Jet Project
item.description: In a major blow to European defense cooperation, Germany and France have abandoned their ambitious FCAS fighter jet project after years of planning and billions in investment.

item.title: South Korea ex-President Yoon sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone plot
item.description: A Seoul court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years in prison for ordering military drones to infiltrate North Korea, intended to create a pretext for his failed December 2024 martial law declaration.

item.title: Trump says "great" Iran settlement will reopen Strait of Hormuz within days
item.description: President Trump announced a "great settlement" with Iran could be signed within days, which would reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end hostilities. Oil prices extended losses on the news.

item.title: Israeli and Palestinian civil society groups meet in France to push for two-state solution
item.description: Israeli and Palestinian civil society representatives will meet in France on Friday, urging the international community not to abandon the two-state solution as Paris works to keep the issue alive amid the broader Middle East conflict.

item.title: Trump cancels planned Iran strikes, says deal "approved" after key points agreed
item.description: US President Donald Trump called off further strikes on Iran and announced that key points of a peace deal have been agreed, with a formal agreement expected within days.
